item.title: Shopkeeper arrested on charge of rape attempt on 12-year boy
item.description: A shopkeeper has allegedly tried to rape a 12-year-old boy inside his shop at Daudkandi in Cumilla....

item.title: Ukrainian strikes kill six in Russia- occupied Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian strikes on Monday killed six people, including two children, in occupied Ukraine, as well as in the Russian border regions of Belgorod and Bryansk, local authorities said....

item.title: Global tensions threaten peacekeeping missions: SIPRI
item.description: Geopolitical tensions and a funding crisis are jeopardising peacekeeping missions, particularly those under the auspices of the United Nations, the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute warned on Monday....

item.title: WHO urges DRC neighbours to act immediately on Ebola
item.description: The countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo are at high risk from Ebola and should act immediately to counter the virus, the World Health Organiation’s head said on Monday....

item.title: Two charred bodies recovered at Badda chips factory
item.description: The police recovered two charred bodies from a chips factory at Badda in Dhaka early Monday....

item.title: Bangladesh Betar withdraws dress code after backlash
item.description: Facing widespread criticism, Bangladesh Betar withdrew its dress code directive for live Facebook bulletins on Sunday....

item.title: BADC renews agreement with Russia for import of MOP fertiliser
item.description: The Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation renewed the existing agreement with the Russian Prodintorg at a hotel in Dhaka on Sunday for the import of 5.95 lakh tonnes of muriate of potash fertiliser....

item.title: IWOC, Dhaka WASA Day Care Centre inaugurated
item.description: President of the Asian Development Bank Masato Kanda and the managing director of Dhaka WASA Md Aminul Islam on Sunday jointly inaugurated the Integrated Water Operation Centre and Dhaka WASA Day Care Centre....

item.title: Chuadanga mango trade booms amid summer heat
item.description: In the intense heat of late summer, mango markets across Chuadanga have come alive again, with orchards steadily supplying seasonal fruit to busy wholesale hubs in the district....

item.title: Nazrul’s birth anniversary observed
item.description: Different organisations, including Dhaka University, Bangla Academy, Bangladesh Udichi Shilpighosti, Bashori, and Chhayanaut, celebrated the 127th birth anniversary of national poet Kazi Nazrul Islam across the country through various programmes on Monday....

item.title: 10 films set to be released on Eid day
item.description: Ten new films are set to be released in cinemas on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha, which will be celebrated in the country on May 28....

item.title: Jamaat to take part in local polls alone
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami will take part in the next local government elections alone -- not with its alliance partners....

item.title: Bangladesh still unprepared for Ebola testing, management
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared a global public health emergency over the Ebola outbreak and urged member states to strengthen preparedness and response measures in line with a set of guidelines....

item.title: Death anniv of Master Iman Ali today
item.description: The 18th death anniversary of Master Iman Ali, former president of Bangladesh Krisak Sangram Samity and also a leader of peasant movement during the era of the British Empire, will be observed today....

item.title: 170 more Bangladeshis return home from Libya
item.description: One hundred and seventy Bangladeshi nationals have been repatriated from Libya....

item.title: BMD forecasts rainy Eid holiday
item.description: Many places in Rangpur, Rajshahi, Mymensingh and Sylhet divisions and a few places in Dhaka, Khulna, Barishal and Chattogram divisions are likely to experience light to moderate rain or thundershowers on Eid-ul-Azha falling on May 28, according to the......

item.title: Millions of pilgrims kick off Hajj
item.description: Over 1.5 million Muslims began filling a vast tent city in the holy city of Makkah on Monday for an annual Hajj pilgrimage carried out against the backdrop of hopes for an end to the war in the Middle East....

item.title: Eid journey ends in tragedy for Naogaon hawkers
item.description: The families at Rajendrabati village of Naogaon’s Manda upazila were preparing to welcome their sons home for Eid-ul-Azha after months of hardship and work in distant districts....

item.title: Excess liquidity in banks surges to Tk 3.78 lakh crore
item.description: Excess liquidity in Bangladesh’s banking sector surged to Tk 3.78 lakh crore in March 2026, marking a 58 per cent rise from a year earlier, as banks continue to hold back from lending amid a soaring defaults, sluggish investment demand and persistent economic uncertainty......

item.title: Dhaka cattle markets hit by torrential rain
item.description: Torrential rains on Sunday and Monday dampened the trading at the capital’s sacrificial animal markets ahead of the Eid-ul-Azha......

item.title: Blacksmiths prepare for Qurbani season
item.description: As Eid-ul-Azha approaches, blacksmiths in the capital are witnessing brisk business as demand for sacrificial tools rises ahead of the festival......

item.title: Clients suffer as ATMs run dry
item.description: Customers have been facing cash withdrawal problems at ATM booths during the Eid-ul-Azha holidays as several banks either ran out of cash or imposed withdrawal limits despite Bangladesh Bank’s directive to ensure uninterrupted ATM services during the seven-day bank closure......

item.title: Subsidy to lead renegotiation over IMF loan
item.description: Volatility in the prices of energy items and fertiliser will continue to require a large subsidy allocation in the 2026–27 national budget and may lead to a renegotiation of the current loan agreement with the International Monetary Fund, finance ministry officials said....

item.title: 15pc factories yet to pay Eid allowance
item.description: About 14.65 per cent or 1,500 factory units out of a total of 10,238 factories haven’t paid the festival allowances ahead of the Eid-ul-Azha, according to the Industrial Police data published on Monday......

item.title: Gold price jumps by Tk 2,158 a bhari
item.description: The Bangladesh Jewellers Association has raised gold prices in the domestic market ahead of Eid-ul-Azha, increasing the price of 22-carat gold by Tk 2,158 per bhari (11.664 grams) to Tk 2,38,121, effective from Monday......

item.title: Dhaka to host confce on trade, investment June 13
Huawei touts new chipmaking tech to sidestep US restrictions
item.description: Chinese tech giant Huawei said on Monday it had developed a new way of making semiconductors that could get around its US-enforced lack of access to the most advanced chipmaking equipment......

item.title: Caution needed to tackle growing measles threat
item.description: THE Eid travel risks a surge in the already critical and highly contagious measles outbreak. As millions of people are headed for outlying areas to celebrate Eid, health experts warn that the viral disease, which has infected more than 72,000 people and killed about 528 children, may spread further. The sharp rise in the test positivity rate, from 54 per cent in early April to more......

item.title: Border landmine threat needs enhanced response
item.description: BANGLADESH’S border on swaths of forests with Myanmar continues to be a killing ground because of landmines planted on the Myanmar side allegedly by the rebel Arakan Army that is said to be controlling the southern Rakhine State, leaving no presence of Myanmar’s Border Guard Police there. In the latest incident that took place on May 24, at least three Bangladeshis......

item.title: Health toll of a drowning coast
item.description: WHEN Cyclone Remal tore through Bangladesh’s southern coast in May 2024, it displaced around 800,000 people in a single night. In the weeks that followed, the crisis did not end with the wind and water. It shifted shape. Across coastal areas, thousands fell ill from contaminated water and mosquito-borne infections. Months later, health workers in Banshkhali and......

item.title: Backlash to women’s visibility
item.description: THE advancement of women has for three decades reflected in various development indices, capturing the attention of the world. From increased female participation in education and their pivotal role in the apparel industry work force to a decline in the maternal mortality rate, their presence is booming in all tiers of governance, from local councils to the parliament......

item.title: Reforming agricultural research system
item.description: NO OTHER sector in Bangladesh has contributed as profoundly to national food security, nutrition security, poverty reduction and rural transformation as agricultural research. The transformation of Bangladesh from a food-deficit nation into one approaching self-sufficiency in food production stands as one of the country’s greatest development achievements......

item.title: Where no child works to survive
item.description: CHILD labour in urban Bangladesh is no longer only a poverty issue. It is a systems failure that we continue to normalise. Despite decades of intervention, 1.78 million children remain engaged in child labour, with more than a million in hazardous conditions. In cities such as Dhaka and Gazipur, this reality is not hidden. It exists in plain sight, woven into the informal......

item.title: Artificial intelligence is misnamed
item.description: FOR centuries, the growth of civilisation has depended on tools that extended the reach of the human mind. The telescope enabled astronomers to peer into distant galaxies. The microscope revealed invisible organisms. The computer transformed complex calculations into routine operations. None of these inventions replaced human intelligence. Each amplified it......

item.title: Russia threatens more strikes on Kyiv, urges foreigners to leave city
item.description: Russia launched scores of drones and missiles at Ukraine over the weekend, killing four people, wounding dozens and causing damage…...

item.title: Lightning strike kills boy, injures brother in Sunamganj
item.description: A 12-year-old boy was killed and his younger brother was critically injured in a lightning strike at Sadar upazila in Sunamganj on Monday afternoon....

item.title: Courts dealing with violence against women, children to remain open during holiday: law minister
item.description: The law, justice and parliamentary affairs minister, Md Asaduzzaman, on Monday said that the government, in consultation with the Supreme Court, decided to keep courts dealing with violence against women and children open during holidays to ensure speedy trial....

item.title: Cattle markets in Rajshahi continue overcharging despite fines
item.description: Leaseholders of several cattle markets in Rajshahi continue to collect excessive tolls from traders and buyers although the district administration has slapped fines on them for overcharging....

item.title: Two people killed in Pirojpur’s Mathbaria
item.description: Two people were killed in separate incidents within two hours at Mathbaria in Pirojpur on Sunday, sparking panic in the area....

item.title: Youth held over raping 71-year-old woman in Chuadanga
item.description: THE police arrested a 20-year-old young man for his alleged involvement in raping a 71-year-old woman in Damurhuda upazila of Chuadanga in the early hours of Monday....

item.title: Ex-Patharghata municipality mayor arrested
item.description: Former Patharghata municipality mayor Anwar Hossain was arrested on early Monday by detective branch police at Patharghata upazila in Barguna district....

item.title: NCP leader Tarek Reza released on bail in Jhenaidah case
item.description: THE National Citizen Party central joint member secretary Tarek Reza was released on bail on Monday morning in a case linked to recent clashes involving political and student groups in Jhenaidah....

item.title: Main Eid congregation at 7:30am at Nat’l Eidgah
item.description: The main Eid-ul-Azha congregation will be held at the national eidgah at 7:30am, with the participation of president Mohammed Shahabuddin and prime minister Tarique Rahman....

item.title: Climate pressure growing on CHT nat’l minority communities: study
item.description: Climate change, environmental degradation and human activities such as deforestation, monoculture plantations and resource extraction are increasing pressure on national minority communities in the Chittagong Hill Tracts, according to a youth-led research......

item.title: Jamaat expresses concern over interference in Islami Bank management
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Monday expressed concern and protested against alleged interferences of the government in Islami Bank Bangladesh....

item.title: Ferry officials removed, fined for overcharging Eid travellers
item.description: State minister for shipping Md Razib Ahsan has directed strict legal action against those responsible for overcharging passengers and other irregularities at the Paturia-Aricha Ferry Terminal during this year’s Eid journey....

item.title: RMG workers block highway at Ashulia for dues
item.description: Workers of a garment factory at Dhaka’s Ashulia industrial belt blocked the Nabinagar-Chandra highway on Monday, demanding payment of arrears....

item.title: 3 DU JCD leaders removed for ‘assaulting’ disabled student
item.description: Three leaders of the Dhaka University unit of Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal have been relieved of their organisational duties over allegations of assaulting a student with disabilities at Jagannath Hall....

item.title: Man sentenced to death over rape of minor girl in Meherpur
item.description: A Meherpur court on Sunday sentenced a man to death over raping a 9-year-old girl in the past year....

item.title: Bangladesh hands over goodwill food consignment to Kuwait in gesture of solidarity
item.description: The Bangladesh government on Sunday symbolically handed over a goodwill consignment of food supplies to the government of Kuwait as a gesture of solidarity and friendship from the people of Bangladesh to the brotherly people of Kuwait....

item.title: BMU outpatient department to remain open on May 27, 30
item.description: Eid congregation will be held at the Central Jame Mosque of Bangladesh Medical University on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha at 7:00am on Thursday....

item.title: Transgender killed, another injured in Narsingdi
item.description: The police recovered the throat slit body of a third gender and rescued another in critical condition in Raipura upazila of Narsingdi on Monday....

item.title: BR launches special train coaches for women for Eid travel
item.description: Bangladesh Railway on Monday launched special coaches for women passengers on selected intercity trains ahead of Eid-ul-Azha to ensure safer and more comfortable travel during the holiday rush....

item.title: Coast Guard seizes 15,000 Yaba pills at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard seized 15,000 Yaba pills worth Tk 75 lakh at Shahpari under Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Sunday night....
